theories.title: Special Theory of Relativity
theories.summary: Revolutionary theory describing the relationship between space and time for objects moving at constant speeds.
theories.description: Special relativity fundamentally changed our understanding of space and time. It introduced the concept that the laws of physics are the same for all observers in uniform motion, and that the speed of light is constant for all observers regardless of their motion. This led to remarkable conclusions about time dilation, length contraction, and the relativity of simultaneity.
keyConcepts: Speed of light as cosmic speed limit

theories.title: General Theory of Relativity
theories.summary: Theory of gravitation describing gravity as the curvature of spacetime caused by mass and energy.
theories.description: General relativity extended special relativity to include gravity and acceleration. Einstein proposed that massive objects curve the fabric of spacetime, and what we perceive as gravity is actually objects following the straightest possible paths (geodesics) through this curved spacetime.

theories.title: Photoelectric Effect
theories.summary: Explanation of how light ejects electrons from metal surfaces, establishing the quantum nature of light.
theories.description: Einstein explained the photoelectric effect by proposing that light consists of discrete packets of energy called photons. This work was instrumental in establishing quantum theory and earned Einstein the Nobel Prize in Physics in 1921.

theories.title: Brownian Motion
theories.summary: Mathematical explanation of random particle motion, providing empirical evidence for the existence of atoms.
theories.description: Einstein's analysis of Brownian motion provided strong evidence for the atomic theory of matter. He showed that the random motion of particles suspended in fluid could be explained by collisions with individual molecules.

theories.title: Bose-Einstein Statistics
theories.summary: Quantum statistics for particles with integer spin, predicting the Bose-Einstein condensate.
theories.description: Working with Indian physicist Satyendra Nath Bose, Einstein developed quantum statistics for particles now called bosons. This work predicted the existence of Bose-Einstein condensates, a state of matter achieved at temperatures near absolute zero.
